DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANTS BRIAN HASSAN, JUSTIN ROBERTS, BAYPOINT BENEFITS & INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE OBJECTIONS WERE NOT WAIVED AS THEY ARE NOT REQUIRED TO BE VERIFIED. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NUMBERS 2 AND 3 AS THEY ARE OVERLY BROAD.THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NUMBER 4. THE RESPONSE WAS ADEQUATE AS IT WAS LIMITED IN TIME. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NUMBERS 5, 7, 8, AND 9 AS THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. THE RESPONSES OTHERWISE MAY BE ADEQUATE. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NUMBER 6 AS THE OBJECTIONS OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN EXCEPT THE CLAIM OF PRIVACY. THE RESPONSE OTHERWISE MAY BE ADEQUATE EXCEPT, AS THERE ARE DOCUMENTS WHICH RELATE TO OTHER EMPLOYEES, THOSE EMPLOYEES' NAMES MAY BE REDACTED. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NUMBERS 10-12. THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. PLAINTIFFS ARE ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NUMBER 13 AS IT IS IRRELEVANT AND INVADES THE RIGHT TO PRIVACY WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS NUMEBRS 1, 2, 3, 4, AND 5. THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. THE RESPONSES OTHERWISE MAY BE ADEQUATE. THE MOTION IS MOOT AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY 50.2 AS A SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSE HAS BEEN SERVED. THE COURT WILL NOT DETERMINE THE ADEQUACY OF THAT RESPONSE ON THIS MOTION. FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE MADE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF ADP, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NUMBER 6, THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITH A LIMITATION TO CURRENT OR FORMER ENTITIES KNOWN TO HAVE BEEN LISTED BY ADP AS POTENTIAL CLIENTS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NUMBER 9, THE RESPONSE IS NOT LIMITED TO LEADS PROVIDED PRIOR TO HASSAN'S AND ROBERTS' DEPARTURE. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NUMBER 11. AS TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NUMBER 5 AND 6, THE MOTION IS GRANTED LIMITED TO CURRENT OR FORMER ENTITIES KNOWN TO HAVE BEEN LISTED BY ADP AS POTENTIAL CLIENTS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS NUMBERS 9 AND 14 AS THE RESPONSES ARE INADEQUATE. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NUMBERS 1, 9, 11, 12, 15, 20, AND 25. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 10 AND 23 AS THEY ARE OVERLY BROAD. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NUMEBRS 28 AND 30, LIMITED TO ENTITIES WHO WERE CLIENTS OF ADP OR LISTED BY ADP AS POTENTIAL CLIENTS. FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE MADE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Nov-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: NO APPEARANCE MADE BY THE PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, ADP, INC.; TODD C. ATKINS, ESQ.(619)231-4725, FOR DEFENDANTS/OPPOSING PARTIES, BRIAN PATRICK HASSAN, ET. AL.
Defendants Brian Hassan, Justin Roberts, Baypoint Benefits & Insurance SErvices, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses is granted in part and denied in part.
The objections were not waived as they are not required to be verified. The motion is denied as to Request for Production Of Documents Numbers 2 and 3 as they are overly broad.The motion is denied as to Request for Production Of Documents Number 4. The response was adequate as it was limited in time. The motion is granted as to Request for Production Of Documents Numbers 5, 7, 8, and 9 as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The responses otherwise may be adequate. The motion is granted as to Request for Production Of Documents Number 6 as the objections overruled and ordered stricken except the claim of privacy. The response otherwise may be adequate except, as there are documents which relate to other employees, those employees' names may be redacted. The motion is granted as to Request for Production Of Documents Numbers 10-12. The objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Plaintiffs are ordered to respond further. The motion is denied as to Request for Production Of Documents Number 13 as it is irrelevant and invades the right to privacy without good cause. The motion is granted as to Request For Admissions Numebrs 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5. The objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The responses otherwise may be adequate. The motion is moot as to Form Interrogatory 50.2 as a supplemental response has been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of that response on this motion. Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. =(EAH/612)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
NO APPEARANCE MADE BY THE PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, ADP, INC.
TODD C. ATKINS, ESQ.(619)231-4725, FOR DEFENDANTS/OPPOSING PARTIES, BRIAN PATRICK HASSAN, ET. AL.

NO NOTICE WAS GIVEN. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff ADP, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Discovery is granted in part and denied in part. As to Special Interrogatory Number 6, the motion is granted with a limitation to current or former entities known to have been listed by ADP as potential clients. The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory Number 9, the response is not limited to leads provided prior to Hassan's and Roberts' departure. The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory Number 11. As to Requests for Admission Number 5 and 6, the motion is granted limited to current or former entities known to have been listed by ADP as potential clients. The motion is granted as to Requests For Admissions Numbers 9 and 14 as the responses are inadequate. The motion is granted as to Requests For Production Numbers 1, 9, 11, 12, 15, 20, and 25. The motion is denied as to Request For Production Nos. 10 and 23 as they are overly broad. The motion is granted as to Request For Production Numebrs 28 and 30, limited to entities who were clients of ADP or listed by ADP as potential clients. Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED (EAH/612)
